source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI via automated Playwright download. Cover page confirms MaidPro Franchise, LLC 2025 FDD.

brand.parent_company: Threshold Brands, LLC / HS Group Holding Company, LLC / The Riverside Company

initial_franchise_fee.notes: Standard Initial Franchise Fee is $45,000, due at signing in lump sum. Non-refundable (except under Franchise Option Program). During 2024, actual fees ranged from $0 to $45,000 due to waivers and discounts. Territory is ~45,000 Qualified Households. Discounts: Military/First Responder (20% off first franchise), Multi-Unit (25% off 2nd+ if 3+ purchased simultaneously), Hard to Serve/Underserved Markets (up to 10% off), Conversion Franchise Program (waives fee entirely, reduces royalty to 2% for first 2 years). Franchise Option Program: fee refunded at opening in exchange for 10% royalty (instead of 6%) for 10 years. Training fee of $500 per additional attendee beyond 2.

provenance.exact_text: You will pay us an initial franchise fee of $45,000 (the "Initial Franchise Fee")... During 2024 our franchise offering included waivers, discounts and refunds such that our Initial Franchise Fees ranged from $0 to $45,000.

royalty_rate.notes: Continuing Royalty is 6% of monthly Gross Consumer Sales. Minimum Royalty of $800/month applies after first 12 months if Gross Consumer Sales fall below $15,000 in any month. Additional Premium Royalty of 2.5% applies on Gross Consumer Sales from customers outside franchisee's territory (with franchisor permission). Conversion franchisees pay reduced 2% royalty for first 2 years (minimum $150/month). Franchise Option Program participants pay 10% royalty for 10 years (in exchange for refunded franchise fee).

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: Single brand fund fee of 2% of Gross Consumer Sales. No additional mandatory local marketing fund contribution as a percentage of sales, but franchisees must spend a minimum of $3,000/month on local advertising for first 24 months (included in Item 7 estimates). After 24 months, Manuals govern local spend amount.

components.name: Brand Fund Fee
components.notes: Current Brand Fund Fee is 2% of monthly Gross Consumer Sales.

components.name: Marketing Services (TMS Connect)
components.notes: Optional. $90/month per territory for Internet Presence support and management. Additional $305/month per territory for SEO services.

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Simpler marketing structure than many competitors. 2% brand fund is the only mandatory percentage-based marketing fee. Local advertising minimum of $3,000/month for first 24 months is a flat dollar amount, not percentage-based.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Brand Fund Fee row, Note 4, Note 7

technology_fee.notes: Technology Fee is $500/month. Waived for second and subsequent MaidPro franchises. Includes MaidPro proprietary cloud-based software (4 user licenses), 4 branded email licenses, MACS marketing/communications tool, and 1-800 customer service number/call routing. Additional software licenses: $25/month each. Additional email licenses: $7-$24/month each depending on storage. MaidPro Go app: optional $10/month. Text messaging: optional $5/month + per-text charges. Fee may increase up to 10% per year (compounding).

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Technology Fee row, MaidPro Software row, Email Licenses row, Notes 3 and 14

transfer_fee.formula: $5,000 flat transfer fee, payable by transferring franchisee.
transfer_fee.notes: Transfer fee is a straightforward $5,000. Significantly lower than many competitors.

other_fees.notes: For each additional person beyond the first two who attend Initial Training.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Additional Employee Initial Training Fee row

other_fees.notes: Registration fee for training programs varies. On-site training: expenses plus $75/hour per employee.
Plus 12% interest per annum (or max rate allowed by law) from date due until paid.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Late Submission Fee row, Note 7

other_fees.notes: Optional service. $35/inquiry for first 20, $32 for next 30, $30 for over 50 inquiries per month.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, National Sales Center row, Note 8

other_fees.notes: Up to $1,500 depending on costs. Non-refundable. Must attend. Does not include travel/lodging/meals. Single fee covers multiple franchises owned.
All amounts franchisor incurs to satisfy a customer complaint if they intervene.
$200/hour per employee assigned plus expenses and attorneys' fees. Only applies if franchisee is in default, absent, incapacitated, or deceased.
Amount equal to then-current initial franchise fee for each Competitive Business plus 7% of that business's gross sales until expiration of non-competition period.
Optional. $35/month per account plus per-transaction fees: credit card 2.99% + $0.29/transaction, e-check 1% of transaction.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Payment Processing Fee row, Note 12

other_fees.notes: Optional alternative to MaidPro Software messaging. $34.99/month per user plus $0.012/text message. Additional phone numbers $5/month each.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Phone and Messaging Services row, Note 13

other_fees.notes: Optional. $90/month per territory for Internet Presence support/management. SEO services also available at $305/month per territory.

line_items.notes: See Item 5 for discounts and waivers. Non-refundable except under Franchise Option Program.

line_items.category: Travel and Living Expenses while Training
line_items.notes: Estimated for 1-2 persons for travel, lodging, meals during on-site training.
General cleaning equipment: washing machine, dryer, vacuum cleaners, cleaning supplies.
Includes computer, software, incorporation fees, etc.

line_items.category: Cost for Initial Vehicle with Wrap
line_items.notes: Low estimate is for lease/financed used vehicle; high is for new vehicle purchase. Must display MaidPro car wrap. Wrap cost included.
Includes required minimum monthly local advertising spend of $3,000 for first 24 months.
Lease approximately 800 sq ft. Anticipated rent of $11-$15/sq ft per year. Must be located within territory.
Required: commercial general liability ($1M/$2M), hired/non-owned auto, workers' comp, third-party bonding ($25K per loss). Varies by state, payroll, and number of employees.
Includes payroll costs, insurance, professional fees, monthly Technology Fee and software licenses.

tables.description: 2024 Gross Consumer Sales and Jobs for all 231 franchised MaidPro outlets open the full year
tables.population: 231 franchised outlets open for entire Relevant Time Period (of 237 total). Excludes 6 outlets opened during 2024 that were not open for the full period.

combined_2_locations.notes: 10 Franchised Businesses that report Gross Consumer Sales from two territories as an aggregate. Excluded from quartile breakdown.

tables.notes: System-wide average Gross Consumer Sales: $461,941. Median: $397,548. Wide range from $14,440 to $2,815,974. Only 42% of outlets at or above average, indicating top-heavy distribution. Gross Consumer Sales do not reflect costs, operating expenses, or other deductions. 7 outlets permanently closed in 2024 are excluded. Territory definition changed in January 2023 to ~45,000 qualified households with $100K+ income (previously varied: 20K-80K households with $75K+ income).

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 is disclosed with a single table showing Gross Consumer Sales and Jobs by quartile for 2024. Provides absolute revenue figures unlike some competitors who only disclose per-unit or per-service metrics. Does not include any cost/expense data. Conversion franchisees and Franchise Option Program participants are included in the data. Territory sizes have varied historically, which affects comparability across franchisees. 7 permanently closed outlets excluded (survivorship bias). No company-owned P&L.

years_reported.notes: All franchised, no company-owned. Net contraction of 5 units. Terminations (13) were the largest exit category. California: 24->22 (3 opened, 5 terminated). Pennsylvania: 15->10 (4 terminated, 1 ceased). New Jersey: 15->14 (1 opened, 2 ceased).

Net contraction of 4 units. Terminations (9) still largest exit category but decreasing. California: 22->19 (1 opened, 1 terminated, 3 ceased). Nevada: 5->2 (3 terminated). Florida: 20->18 (1 opened, 2 terminated, 1 ceased).

System stable: net change of 0 units. Terminations (4) lowest in 3 years. Maryland: 8->5 (3 terminated). Michigan: 4->3 (1 ceased). New York: 7->6 (1 ceased). Projected openings for next fiscal year: 4 new franchised outlets (CO, CT, FL x2) plus 2 signed agreements not yet opened (IL, OH). Table 1 in FDD contains a typographical error: third row labeled '2023' should read '2024' based on context and Table 3 totals.

action_types.notes: MaidPro Franchise, LLC v. La Lucha, Inc., Mariusz Mastalerz, and Lorraine Marques, AAA Case No. 01-24-0003-3646. Filed March 27, 2024. Arbitration alleging former franchisee failed to pay amounts due and operated a competing maid services business in violation of post-termination non-compete. Resolved by settlement on June 30, 2024: franchisee paid amounts owed and agreed to abide by non-compete.

item_3_litigation.notes: Single arbitration action, initiated by franchisor, resolved by settlement within 3 months. Light litigation profile. No regulatory actions, no pending matters.

provenance.exact_text: our former affiliate, MaidPro, Inc., provided residential and commercial cleaning services from 1993 to 2020... We have been offering franchises since February 1, 1997.

warnings: SYSTEM CONTRACTION (stabilizing): MaidPro shrank for 2 years then stabilized (246->241->237->237). Net loss of 9 units over 3 years (-3.7%), but 2024 showed zero net change.

Table 1 in FDD contains a typographical error: the third row is labeled '2023' but contextually represents 2024 data (start=237, end=237, net change=0). This is confirmed by Table 3 state-by-state totals for 2024.
Item 19 does not disclose costs or expenses — only Gross Consumer Sales and number of jobs. Profitability cannot be assessed from FDD data alone.
Territory definition changed January 2023 (~45K qualified households at $100K+ income), making historical comparisons across franchisees complex.
Franchise Option Program allows $0 franchise fee in exchange for 10% royalty (vs. 6%) for 10 years — creates two distinct fee structures within the system.
Conversion franchisees pay reduced 2% royalty for first 2 years — further variation in royalty structures.
7 outlets that permanently closed in 2024 are excluded from Item 19 data — survivorship bias applies.
Wide revenue dispersion: Q1 average ($958K) is 7.1x Q4 average ($134K). Bottom quartile average of $134K may not support franchise fee obligations.
In the last 3 years, current and former franchisees signed provisions restricting their ability to speak openly about their experience — due diligence caveat.
An independent franchisee association (Maid Together Franchisee Association Inc.) exists, which may indicate franchisee-franchisor tensions.
PE-backed: MaidPro is owned through Threshold Brands / HS Group Holding Company / The Riverside Company (private equity). Financial statements are for parent HS Group Holding Company, LLC.

null_fields.reason: Fee is per-inquiry with tiered pricing ($30-$35/inquiry). No single value.
Variable — all amounts franchisor incurs. No fixed value.
Formula-based: current franchise fee + 7% of competitive business gross sales. No single value.
